title: No evidence of tumor cluster at Newton-Wellesley Hospital after 11 reported tumors in nurses, external review finds
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The review found that the hospital's facility did not cause the tumors.

Newton-Wellesley Hospital Pat Greenhouse/Globe Staff An external review concluded there is no evidence of a workplace tumor cluster at Newton-Wellesley Hospital after 11 nurses working on the same floor reported being diagnosed with brain tumors.

According to the report published March 30, Stefanos Kales, a professor of medicine at Harvard Medical School, concluded that “the overall hospital environment is safe” and that the reported cases do not appear to represent a true workplace tumor cluster.

title: Rafael Devers’ disastrous tenure with Giants hits new low with pinch-hit drama
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox Devers initially refused to leave Sunday's game after getting replaced with a pinch runner in the ninth inning.
Rafael Devers didn't hide his frustrations with the Giants on Sunday.

Saul Martinez/Getty Images By Conor Ryan June 22, 2026 | 7:53 PM 3 minutes to read Two things can be true when it comes to the Red Sox and their decision to trade their former slugger, Rafael Devers, to the Giants last June.

title: Report: Bucks eyeing ‘prized’ Celtics rookie Hugo Gonzalez for Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics Gonzalez is viewed as having a bright future despite falling out of Boston's playoff rotation.
Hugo Gonzalez, who the Celtics took with the 28th pick of last year's draft, showed some impressive flashes in his age 19 season.
Finn Gomez for the Boston Globe As the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade talks continue to intensify, Milwaukee has indicated interest in adding one of the Celtics’ promising young players.

title: Andover middle schooler dies a month after being pulled from pond
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The 14-year-old was found submerged in about 10 to 12 feet of water, authorities say.
An eighth-grade student at Doherty Middle School in Andover has died after he was pulled from a pond in Harold Parker State Forest last month, school officials announced.

Cody Nguyen, 14, died Friday following a “medical emergency” at Field Pond in May, Doherty Middle School Principal Rebecca Perry and Andover Public Schools Interim Superintendent Keith Taverna said in a message to the school community Saturday.

title: Mariano defends House bill that narrows scope of audit law
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Politics Tensions are still running high between House Speaker Ron Mariano and State Auditor Diana DiZoglio.
House Speaker Ron Mariano.

Suzanne Kreiter/Globe Staff Before the state House of Representatives passed a bill earlier this month that would narrow the scope of the legislative audit long sought by State Auditor Diana DiZoglio, House Speaker Ron Mariano delivered a floor speech where he advocated for the bill and criticized DiZoglio.
